_doc: Casebook judgments. Two-pass authoring per AGENTS.md §Casebook authoring. Schema per entry: { id, chapter_unlock, draft, tags: { article[], principle[], context[] }, principle_moves[]: { id, effectiveness_modifiers, cost, name (Design), flavor_line (Design) }, judgment_name (Design), case_summary (Design) }. Tag values must exist in data/tag_taxonomy.json. draft:true entries are not loaded by casebook.gd until both Code and Design passes are complete.

judgments._doc: Beat 12 procedural-reset judgment. Triggers chapter1.court_won_procedural_reset via judge_district_ch1.json remedy_round_3_<stance> on_dismiss. Unlocked as a Casebook entry after court victory. Conditions: casebook_judge_state reaches 'round_3_remedy' AND at least one bonus_evidence_collected item is present OR all three rounds are won without bonus evidence (modest-remedy discipline — the remedy is always procedural, never a full merits win). The actual remedy text lives in judge_district_ch1.json; do not duplicate it here.

conditions._doc: Judgment 'fires' (is added to Casebook) when all conditions are met. casebook_judge_state must have reached round_3_remedy. court_won_procedural_reset is set true by judge_district_ch1.json on_dismiss; this entry is unlocked at that moment. bonus_evidence_collected is optional — its presence affects argument strength during the rounds but not whether the judgment is awarded.

bonus_evidence_variants._doc: Bonus evidence in chapter1.bonus_evidence_collected changes the flavor of the Round 3 argument but not the judgment outcome. All three paths award the same judgment.
bonus_evidence_variants.wojcik_witness_statement: Round 3 sympathetic carrier — affidavit adds tenancy-validity texture to the rehearing-remedy argument.
bonus_evidence_variants.return_to_sender_slip: Round 3 blunt-procedural carrier — slip strengthens the wrongful-service finding underlying the remedy.
bonus_evidence_variants.lease_1962_inheritance_1987: Round 3 technical carrier — historical paperwork adds underlying tenancy weight; one-clause acknowledgment from the bench that tenancy is not before the court.

judgments.remedy_discipline: Modest procedural reset only. The client remains (procedurally, for now). The firm receives the 5,000 PLN Sikorska fee. The hearing is rescheduled; the merits remain open. Bonus evidence may strengthen argument strength but cannot upgrade the remedy itself beyond the procedural reset.

principle_moves.name: Motion to Set Aside
principle_moves.flavor_line: Service of process is the threshold. Below it, there is no hearing. There was no hearing.

principle_moves.name: Actual Notice Window
principle_moves.flavor_line: The fourteen-day clock runs from actual notice, not from the defective delivery. We are inside that window. The statute says so.

principle_moves.name: Third-Clause Non-Cure
principle_moves.flavor_line: The current occupant of number seven is not her authorized agent. The landlord cannot bootstrap service through a stranger to the tenancy.

principle_moves.name: Rehearing as Remedy
principle_moves.flavor_line: The remedy is modest. We do not ask for a merits finding. We ask for the hearing she was entitled to before any of this began.

judgments.case_summary: A misdirected eviction notice — sent to an address renumbered eleven years before, by a landlord whose 2019 countersignature proved he knew — entitled the client to a set-aside and a fresh hearing she had never received.
judgments.memory_hook: The address was old. The landlord's signature was new. The gap between those two facts was the case.
